TextENGNew: ...At first, I truly had intended to leave quickly.
And yet, I... in the end......
Gentle sunlight streamed through the transoms along the veranda and into the room, bathing everything in a pleasant warmth.
changeReason: Japanese うららか means 'gentle/soft'; new translation corrects 'bright sunlight' to 'gentle sunlight', changing the atmospheric quality described.

TextENGNew: Lying on my side with my cheek pressed against the tatami, the scent and texture of the rush grass were so soothing that a desire to simply drift off to sleep welled up within me.
``...Oh, that will not do.``
To fall asleep in the middle of the day would be the height of indolence... With that thought, I forced my drooping eyelids open, stretched my limbs wide, and drew a deep breath.
``--Riku, is the midday meal not ready yet?``
``Coming~!@k@vS16/46/DS43020116. Just a moment longer, please~!``
changeReason: Japanese は～い is a response meaning 'coming!' rather than 'soon!'; new translation corrects the nature of Riku's reply.

TextENGNew: ``Mm... very well.``
Hearing Riku's reply from the kitchen, I rolled over once more onto the tatami in the living room.
A faint, delicious aroma wafted in... Was it vegetables, or perhaps meat?
I opened my eyes and gazed up at the ceiling. The wood was still white, as though freshly built, with the grain forming elegant patterns.
And not a single cobweb or speck of dust could be found anywhere -- likely a testament to Riku's fastidious nature.
...When I strained my ears, I could hear the cascading song of cicadas pouring down like rain.
Every moment spent in this household, every experience, felt so fresh... so endlessly fascinating.
``...And yet, what on earth am I doing here...?``
Indeed... on that day, it had been perfectly fine up to the point of accepting Riku's invitation and imposing upon the Furude household.
changeReason: Old translation implies visiting caused problems; new translation correctly conveys that accepting the invitation itself was fine, matching Japanese そう…あの日…よかった structure.

TextENGNew: However--
``Since you have come all this way, why not stay for dinner?``
``It has gotten quite late. Why not stay the night?``
``There is no rush to leave before your wound has healed, is there?``
And so, detained by one invitation after another... before I knew it, my stay in this household had stretched to well over a month.
``Caring for God is the duty of one who serves at this shrine.``
I was grateful that he said this while so diligently attending to my meals and personal needs.
But... did this man mistake me for some sort of zashiki-warashi, a household spirit of good fortune, and mean to keep me confined here?
My right foot had, of course, healed completely long ago. The wound had not been serious to begin with, so it was hardly remarkable once the bleeding had been stopped and the wound disinfected.
And yet... in the end, I never once used the ointment I had brought with me.
Naturally, Ryuun-Ohc's specially formulated ointment would have healed the wound almost perfectly in an instant, far surpassing those barely effective natural herbs.
However...
``All right.@k@vS16/46/DS43020122. Now then, please hold out your foot, Hanyuu.``
``D-do not trouble yourself!@k@vS16/45/DS43070412. I can manage the bandaging myself...!``
``Oh, that will not do.@k@vS16/46/DS43020124. You must grind the herbs more thoroughly and apply them evenly over the entire wound...``
``Q-quiet!@k@vS16/45/DS43070414. This is how I have always done it, so do not complain!``
``Now now, Hanyuu, that is not right.@k@vS16/46/DS43020126. The bandage must be wrapped like this...``
``...You truly, truly do not listen to a word anyone says, do you?``
``Ahahaha!``
``Do not laugh!``
``Haah... honestly...``
I pictured Riku's gentle face as he fussed over a single foot wound as though I were family... and let out yet another exasperated sigh.
And yet... why was it? It did not displease me in the slightest.
If anything, grumbling complaints while Riku tended to me was... in its own way, even comforting.
And so, when the wound finally healed... I felt, for reasons I could not fathom, a twinge of something like disappointment...
``What has gotten into me...?``
After what Ryuun-Ohc had said to me the other day, I had thought this stay might serve to heal my body and spirit.
And yet... I could not make sense of what I felt.
Whenever I spoke with Riku, I became strangely irritated and emotional.
My face would flush,@k my heart would pound wildly,@k until it was nearly suffocating...@k and yet I continued to meet with him, to talk, to exchange all manner of words.
And now, bringing this to an end had itself become something I could no longer bring myself to do...
changeReason: Old translation says the end is 'in sight' and she feels reluctant; new translation says she can no longer bring herself to end it at all, matching the Japanese 億劫になってしまっていた (became unable/unwilling).

TextENGNew: ``Nothing like this... has ever happened to me before...``
Honestly, spending time with others was something I had never been good at.
I did not know how the other Putus felt, but being in a group always made me oddly uncomfortable, and I had the sense that I always kept my distance from their circles.
Even with Shezhen, the late Jedha who had served alongside me in this island nation, I could scarcely recall speaking to her beyond what was strictly necessary.
So why was it that I felt none of that discomfort or reluctance with Riku...?
``--Hanyuu.``
Just then--

As if to sever my train of thought, a voice came from the corridor beyond the sliding door. When I sat up and turned to look, Riku's silhouette was indeed visible on the other side of the paper screen.
``I am coming in, if that is all right?``
``Hm? Oh, y-yes...``
Upon my answer, the door slid quietly open and Riku entered the room.
In his hands he carried a pot emanating a wonderful aroma -- most likely some kind of stew.
He hung the pot over the sunken hearth, then returned briefly to the kitchen before coming back with dishes and a rice container arranged on a tray.
``...Sorry to keep you waiting, Hanyuu.@k@vS16/46/DS43020131. Now then, let us eat.``
``A hunter shared some boar meat with me today, so I prepared a chikuzen-ni stew.@k@vS16/46/DS43020133. Do you like that sort of thing?``
``Chikuzen-ni, you say... I am not familiar with it.@k@vS16/45/DS43070424. What manner of dish is it?``
``Ah, well, you see...``
Riku explained what chikuzen-ni was, beaming all the while.
...Unfortunately, having no knowledge whatsoever of cooking, I could not follow a word of it.
``In any case, why not simply try it first?@k@vS16/46/DS43020136. Is there anything you dislike?``
``Nothing in particular.@k@vS16/45/DS43070426. ...Ah, but please, no spicy vegetables.@k@vS16/45/DS43070427. They make me feel ill.``
``Understood.``
With that, Riku ladled the 'chikuzen-ni' into a small bowl and set it on the tray before me.
He then set out his own portion and rice for the two of us, laid out the chopsticks, and took his seat facing me.
...I could tell before even tasting it. The aroma was wonderful, and in the words Riku had taught me, it looked 'delicious.'
``Here you are. Thank you for waiting.``
``Very well. I shall partake...@k@vS16/45/DS43070429. hm?``
Riku's hand reached out before me and gently came to rest upon my right hand, which held the chopsticks. Wondering what he was up to, I turned to find him wearing a smile of gentle exasperation.
changeReason: Japanese そっと添えられる means 'gently placed alongside'; old translation uses 'grabbed' which implies force, while new correctly conveys the gentle nature of Riku's gesture.

TextENGNew: ``What is it, Riku?``
``...Hanyuu.@k@vS16/46/DS43020140. Have I not told you many times now? That is not how a lady speaks.``
``Now then, once more from the top.``
``R-right...``
Though I bristled slightly at being corrected as though I were a small child, I resignedly collected myself and reluctantly reshaped my words.
Let me see, in this situation I was supposed to... set down the chopsticks, bring my hands together, and...
``Th-thank you for the meal...@k@vS16/45/DS43070434. I-is that acceptable?``
``...'Is that acceptable?'``
``I-is that... acceptable?``
``Yes, well done.``
I could not tell whether he was praising me or mocking me.
But I felt the corners of my lips threatening to lift ever so slightly, so I hastily looked away.
...It had started just a few days ago.
During one of our idle conversations, Riku had casually remarked that my manner of speaking was 'not very feminine.'
Upon reflection, I had learned the language of this land from male Ryuun, beginning with the elders. And so it seemed I had been speaking in masculine terms all along without ever realizing it.
My guard had been down at the time, however... and I carelessly responded by asking 'What do you mean?'
...That had been a grave mistake.
``How about it? Would you like to give it a try?``
``I, well, that is...@k@vS16/45/DS43070438. I see nothing wrong with how I speak now...``
``It will be perfectly fine, it is only a very simple adjustment.@k@vS16/46/DS43020146. I am certain it will make you even more lovely than you already are.``
I had tried frantically to take it back, but with someone who hardly listened to others in the first place, it was already far too late.

And so I found myself all but coerced into 'reluctantly' receiving instruction in feminine speech from Riku, and with Ryuun-Ohc's assistance, I was gradually acquiring a manner of speaking that was somewhat more 'ladylike'...

``In the first place, tiresome as it may be to repeat--@k@vS16/45/DS43070441. I mean, am I not essentially a deity to this village?@k@vS16/45/DS43070442. So why must I go to such lengths--@k@vS16/45/DS43070443. ...I mean, why must I do all this?``
changeReason: Japanese くどいようだが means 'tiresome as it may be to repeat'; old translation's 'not to be a jerk' and 'ungrateful' misrepresent the self-correcting speech pattern where Hanyuu practices feminine language mid-sentence.

TextENGNew: ``Now now, polite speech is far more becoming of a lady.@k@vS16/46/DS43020148. Your resolute way of speaking has its own charm, certainly, but a more feminine manner would only enhance your appeal, you know.``
``Either way, it hardly makes a difference in daily life--@k@vS16/45/DS43070445. does it?``
Besides, I honestly had no obligation to change my speech. And even if I did, there was scarcely any benefit in it for me.
Even so... Riku saw me as a woman. Me, with my horns, a being not even of this world.
And so, though I could never bring myself to say it to his face... that made me feel truly... glad.
Until now, I had gone through life with absolutely no awareness of being a 'woman.'
No... more precisely, aside from moments when I felt the difference in physical strength, I had not even thought myself different from a man.
But ever since meeting Riku, I had slowly, gradually begun to recover an awareness of my own sex that I had all but forgotten.
And the secret wish I had harbored all along -- to be seen not as a demon, nor as a god, nor as anything else, but simply as 'myself,' a being dwelling in this world -- had been fulfilled by him...
I felt... yes, that was it. I felt happy.
``... (munch, munch)``
``How is it? The taste?``
``...It is deli... delicious.``
That was neither flattery nor a lie. It was my honest impression.
...Until now, operating on the principle that nutrients were nutrients regardless of preparation, I had never done anything to process my food.
At most, I would cut it into manageable pieces or heat it to sterilize it.
But... I had been utterly ignorant of how much satisfaction a meal prepared with care could bring to the one who ate it.
``Oh, Hanyuu.@k@vS16/46/DS43020151. You have gotten quite good with chopsticks.``
``I-is that so...?@k@vS16/45/DS43070450. I mean, is that really so...?``
``Indeed.@k@vS16/46/DS43020153. You can grip the food quite well now, can you not?``
``...Something this simple can be mastered after only a few attempts.``
Feeling vaguely annoyed, I looked away and brought a cube of boar meat to my mouth.
When I bit down, sweet juices flooded across my palate, and as I chewed and swallowed, a profound sense of fulfillment spread through me.
...To begin with, I had intentionally reduced the act of eating to its barest essentials.
When I consumed food, the process of digesting organic matter produced waste within my body, which, much like injuries, interfered with my ability to transform into my astral body.
The ability to shift into astral form was a decisive advantage in combat against the Grifys, greatly influencing the outcome of battle.
And so, having spent my entire life immersed in warfare, I had kept my meals to a bare minimum as a precaution... rarely eating more than a handful of nuts or berries.
changeReason: Japanese 木の実 means 'nuts/tree nuts'; old translation incorrectly renders this as 'fruit' while new correctly translates as 'nuts or berries'.

TextENGNew: But now...
...What was it, I wondered.
This warm, comfortable air.
I had never imagined that having someone beside me, eating food they had prepared, could feel this deeply satisfying.
Being enveloped in this atmosphere, I felt as though my entire body had grown weightless, floating...
``?@k@vS16/46/DS43020155. Is something the matter, Hanyuu?``
``...It is nothing.@k@vS16/45/DS43070454. Another helping, Riku.``
And yet, in sharp contrast, a strange, contradictory blend of irritation and agitation surged within me, and the heat spreading from my cheeks to my neck was almost unbearable...
...After lunch, once Riku had finished cleaning up, he prepared tea for me.
``...Here you are, Hanyuu.``
``Ah... thank you.``
I tilted the teacup lightly and took a sip, and the rich aroma brought an involuntary smile to my face.
Tea after a meal was quite the luxury, but Riku had apparently prevailed upon a traveling merchant the previous month to sell him some, claiming it would aid the healing of my wound.
...Even though my wound had long since vanished without a trace.
``Lovely weather again today.@k@vS16/46/DS43020158. The breeze feels wonderful...``
Riku and I sat side by side on the veranda, gazing out at the small vegetable garden in the yard.
As the head priest of the shrine, Riku essentially lived off the agricultural produce and monetary offerings donated by the villagers, so he had no need to work the fields.
...I was somewhat impressed that someone so young had earned such considerable trust from the community.

Even so, he told me he preferred not to rely too heavily on others' generosity, wanting to do what he could for himself. And so he made his living from the vegetables he grew in this small garden and the fish he caught.
Idly sipping the now-tepid tea, I stole a glance at Riku's profile.
Despite his typically breezy manner, his features retained a certain guilelessness -- an innocence that seemed youthful even for a human of roughly twenty years.
And above all else, deep within those eyes, a certain radiance...
There dwelt in them a purity so crystalline it was nearly blinding -- something entirely unlike sharpness or cunning.
``Well then...@k@vS16/46/DS43020160. I shall be off to sweep the shrine grounds.``
``?@k@vS16/46/DS43020162. Is something the matter...?``
``N-no, it is nothing...``
...I had been staring so intently that Riku's sudden movement caught me off guard. I quickly averted my gaze so he would not see my face.
Sweeping, was it...
``...Um, Riku.@k@vS16/45/DS43070461. Shall I help you?``
For an instant, Riku blinked at me in bewilderment, as though he had not understood what I said.
The moment I saw that reaction, I deeply regretted the words that had tumbled out of me unbidden... but taking them back now would be even more humiliating, so I pressed on.
``I-I said...@k@vS16/45/DS43070463. I would like to help you with your work as well...@k@vS16/45/DS43070464. if you would allow me.``
``O-oh... well, but...``
Riku scratched his head with a troubled look, his gaze drifting uncertainly.
...Surely he did not think me incapable?
``It is merely sweeping, is it not?@k@vS16/45/DS43070466. One takes a broom and sweeps away the fallen leaves and dust.@k@vS16/45/DS43070467. Even I am capable of that much.``
``Ah, no, that is not what I meant.@k@vS16/46/DS43020166. As I have mentioned before, you are the guardian deity of this village...``
``...If that is your reasoning, then there are a great many other matters I would like to see rectified first.``
``Huh?@k@vS16/46/DS43020168. Ahahaha, you have me there.``
...This is no laughing matter, you wretch. Shall I truly curse you?
``In any case, you are still my guest.@k@vS16/46/DS43020170. I could hardly ask you to help with household chores.``
``Be that as it may, I have been imposing upon your hospitality for over a month now.@k@vS16/45/DS43070470. The least I can do is repay you in some small way, or I shall not be at ease.``
``I appreciate the thought, truly...@k@vS16/46/DS43020172. but during the daytime, it would still be best to refrain.``
And then, at last, I grasped the reason for Riku's halting manner -- and what his gaze had been drawn to.
Of course... my horns.
I had by no means forgotten them, but... how careless of me.
He had been worried about others seeing them...
``...I truly am a burden, am I not?``
``N-not at all!@k@vS16/46/DS43020174. Never, not in the least!``

``Ever since my father was appointed to his post in the castle town, I have been living here alone for quite some time.@k@vS16/46/DS43020176. So spending time with you like this brings me genuine joy!@k@vS16/46/DS43020177. It is only...``
``...Only what?``
``...@k@vS16/46/DS43020179. You may scorn me for saying this.``

``From the moment we first met, I have never once seen you as anything resembling an evil person.@k@vS16/46/DS43020181. But I cannot guarantee that the villagers, who know nothing of you, would not hurl cruel words at you on sight.@k@vS16/46/DS43020182. ...That is what I cannot bear.``
The people of this village were, to put it plainly, insular... They would direct gazes full of prejudice and suspicion at any stranger without a second thought.
Most likely, the lingering memory of the attack by outside demons was what made them this way.
Deeply suspicious, constantly on guard... those were the defining traits of the majority of Onigafuchi Village's residents.
And so I agreed that Riku's concern was well-founded... Or rather, I was somewhat surprised to learn he had been thinking of me to such a degree.
However--
``...Even so, Riku.@k@vS16/45/DS43070476. The villagers already know of my presence here, do they not?``
``...How did you know that?``
``...So I was right, then.``
In truth, I had been bluffing with no certainty whatsoever... but he was too good-natured -- too incapable of deception.
Given that this was a shrine, the comings and goings of visitors were nearly constant.
And so anyone with keen enough senses might, given the right circumstances, notice that someone other than Riku was living in this house.
And if the village's chief priest was harboring someone in his estate, it was only human nature to want to know who that someone was.
``If being seen is truly such a problem, then perhaps I should assume my 'astral body,' which is invisible to the human eye?``
``Huh?@k@vS16/46/DS43020185. You can do that?``
``...Ah, but not at present.@k@vS16/45/DS43070480. I cannot transform while there is food in my stomach...``
...But then again, if I remained in astral form, I would be unable to eat a single bite of Riku's cooking.
It was pathetic to admit, but now that I had tasted those comforts, I dearly wished to avoid that.
``...Oh, I have it.``
``Please wait just a moment.@k@vS16/46/DS43020188. I have something that may help.``
changeReason: Japanese いいものがあります means 'I have a good item/thing'; old translation renders it as 'a good idea' while new correctly indicates a physical object.

TextENGNew: Riku hurried back to the living room and dragged a large wicker chest from the depths of the closet.
changeReason: Japanese 行李 refers to a wicker trunk/chest; 'portmanteau' in the old translation is a different type of luggage. New translation uses 'wicker chest' which is more accurate.

TextENGNew: He opened the lid before my puzzled gaze and pulled out a bundle of white cloth.
``...What is that?``
``A Shugendo monk who stopped by here some time ago left these behind.@k@vS16/46/DS43020190. I washed and stored them in case he returned for them, but...``
``Hanyuu?``
``If you would, please hold still for a moment.``
With that brief word, Riku unfolded the cloth and draped it carefully over my head, taking care not to catch it on my hair.
He then held up a hand mirror from the chest and asked, 'How does it look?'
I turned my head this way and that, checking the effect from multiple angles. ...Both horns were neatly concealed. Not bad at all.

``It may be somewhat inconvenient, but... would you be willing to wear this hood while you are here?@k@vS16/46/DS43020194. I shall tell the villagers that you wear it because of a scar on your head.``
``...And that will not cause trouble for you?``
``None whatsoever.``
Riku nodded back at me with a radiant smile.
Looking at that face, I found myself genuinely believing that everything would indeed be all right... and before I knew it, I had answered '...Very well.'

``Ah, but if we are going that far... it would probably be best to change your clothes to something from the shrine as well.@k@vS16/46/DS43020197. Your current attire would draw too much attention otherwise...``
``Is that so...?@k@vS16/45/DS43070487. I mean, is that the case?``
At Riku's suggestion, I looked down and took stock of my own appearance.
These garments were the armored vestments worn by a Hai-Ryuun, specialized for combat against the Grifys.
Though they appeared to be ordinary cloth at a glance, they were designed to absorb a measure of slashing and impact damage, while offering excellent mobility so as not to hinder combat.

For as long as I could remember, wearing these had felt perfectly natural... but it was true that compared to the clothing of the villagers and Riku himself, they were distinctly different -- conspicuously so.
``With that in mind, Hanyuu.@k@vS16/46/DS43020199. Would you be willing to wear clothing I have here at the shrine?``
``...Very well.@k@vS16/45/DS43070489. If I am to remain in this village for an extended time, such a measure may well be necessary.@k@vS16/45/DS43070490. Oh, but...``
After nodding to Riku's proposal, a certain problem occurred to me.
If I recalled correctly, Riku had lost his mother at a young age... and had been living alone since his father left to serve at a distant posting.
In such a bachelor's household, where would women's clothing even come from...?
``Heh heh heh heh... You need not worry about that in the slightest, Hanyuu.``
``...Pardon?``
``The truth is!@k@vS16/46/DS43020202. Anticipating just such an occasion, I have been making thorough preparations!@k@vS16/46/DS43020203. Now then, behold!``
And with that, wearing an expression of burning enthusiasm, Riku threw open the closet door on the far side of the living room.
And inside was--
``...WH-WHAAAAAAAAAAT?!``
The closet was filled with an uncountable number of garments.
And every last one of them was adorned in red and white... Miko outfits?

``What do you think, Hanyuu?@k@vS16/46/DS43020205. I have a full selection, each tailored in a different style to suit every possible taste!@k@vS16/46/DS43020206. And naturally, they have all been washed, maintained, and treated against insects!``
``Furthermore, each design has been modified to cater to individual preferences, so please feel free to choose whichever strikes your fancy!``
``Go on!``
``Oh my, Hanyuu -- rendered speechless by sheer admiration?@k@vS16/46/DS43020210. ...No no, please, do not hold back.``

``As a devotee of miko attire beyond all else--@k@vS16/46/DS43020212. that is to say, as a servant of the divine, and as the one charged with extending you the finest hospitality, I have merely done what comes naturally.``
``N-no... that is not the issue...``
...Squeezing out even that much was the very limit of what I could manage.

It was true that even in an all-male household, there could be any number of reasons to possess women's clothing -- a keepsake of his mother, preparations for unforeseen circumstances, and so forth. Nothing inherently strange about it.
But no matter how one looked at it, the number of miko outfits crammed into that closet was ten... no, twenty... no, assuredly more than that.

Moreover, judging from the sheen of the fabric and their pristine, unblemished condition... it was utterly inconceivable that they had simply been left sitting here for years. They were, without question, 'as good as new.'
(...Wait, hold on, hold on, hold on, hold ON!@k@vS16/45/DS43070496. Just one moment!!)
Miko outfits were the formal vestments worn by women who served at a shrine. But naturally, there was no one at Furude Shrine who could be called a miko.
...Perhaps one existed somewhere I was unaware of, but at the very least, I had never seen one.
``H-hey... Ryuun-Ohc?``
``Yes, what is it, Jedha?``
Almost unconsciously, I drew the sword at my waist close to my lips, established a mental link with Ryuun-Ohc through the charged crystal on the hilt, and sent my words to her.
``...@k@vS16/45/DS43070499. Ryuun-Ohc, tell me.@k@vS16/45/DS43070500. How long has it been... since there was last a miko at this village?``
``One moment, searching my records...@k@vS16/00/DS43050144. done.``
``? ...What is the matter?``
``...Jedha.@k@vS16/00/DS43050146. Are you... certain you wish to hear the answer?``
``Out with it.``
``Approximately... 186 years ago.@k@vS16/00/DS43050148. Incidentally, as the late wife of the Furude head priest was not herself a priestess, she did not wear miko attire.``
``Furthermore, while a number of women in this village have performed menial work for the shrine... none were priestesses, and thus none wore miko attire.``
``For formal rituals, it appears that a suitable number of miko are dispatched from the nation's head shrine.``
``In other words... what exactly are you telling me?``
``I-I suppose one would have to say it is... a personal hobby? A collection, of sorts...``
My head throbbed. ...In all likelihood, Ryuun-Ohc had arrived at much the same conclusion I had, with only minor differences.
Which meant... this man was, in fact...?
``You see, miko attire was created for the pure maidens who serve the gods -- it is nothing less than art given form!``
``Among all the women born in a given time and place, only a select handful are worthy of wearing it!``
``Indeed, in some generations there may be none at all -- none whatsoever! Only the rarest, most precious few are granted that honor!``
``Wait, you--``
``And in this village, alas, there has been an utter absence of women suited to wearing miko attire!``
``...Well, perhaps Shino-san, the village head's daughter, might barely qualify?@k@vS16/46/DS43020218. No, perhaps even that would be too generous?``
``A thought so rude that Shino-san would not merely half-kill but outright obliterate me if she ever heard it -- and yet such is the depth of the barren, endless winter we have endured!``
``You mean the winter you have endured, not 'we,' do you not...?``

``However!@k@vS16/46/DS43020221. At long last, a savior has appeared before us!@k@vS16/46/DS43020222. A radiant goddess thought to exist only in myths and fairy tales!@k@vS16/46/DS43020223. And that goddess is you, Hanyuu!!``
``Would you listen to me for once in your life!@k@vS16/45/DS43070509. ...Wait, fweh?!``
And then, the fervor of his impassioned speech still blazing, Riku seized both my hands and peered into my face with the most earnest expression I had ever seen him wear.
The sheer intensity of it made me recoil instinctively... and as those inexplicable palpitations hammered violently against my chest... I barely managed to force words out.
``S-so... what is it you are asking me to do?``
``--Please wear a miko outfit.@k@vS16/46/DS43020225. Choose any one you like from among these!``
``Go on!``
``R-right...``
I had never imagined anyone could speak so fervently -- no, so zealously -- over a single article of clothing. But I had already agreed, so I stepped into the closet.
In any case, the red-and-white color scheme of the miko outfits bore a faint resemblance to my armored vestments. I would likely be able to wear one without too much discomfort.
With that thought, I selected one at random and picked it up...
Now wait just a moment.
changeReason: Japanese おい待て、コラ is a stern 'hey, wait a moment' expression; old translation's 'What. The. Actual. Hell.' adds profanity not present in the original.

TextENGNew: ``? What is it, Hanyuu?``
``...Riku.@k@vS16/45/DS43070513. There is something I am desperately curious about... This is a miko outfit, correct?``
``Why yes, it is. What of it?``
``...Then why--``
``Why does the neckline plunge this low?!@k@vS16/45/DS43070516. One's chest would be entirely exposed!``
``Precisely!@k@vS16/46/DS43020230. That is its defining feature, if you please!``
``YOU FOOOOOOOL!!!!``
Having finally reached the very end of my patience, I drove my fist squarely into Riku's chest.
...To a human like him, it carried the force of a devastating blow, and his body sailed through the air like a leaf on the wind.
He crashed through the paper door on the far side of the room, tumbled off the veranda, and rolled all the way into the garden.
``~~!!@k@vS16/45/DS43070519. This one too!@k@vS16/45/DS43070520. And this one!@k@vS16/45/DS43070521. And this one as weeeell!!!``
I snatched up the neatly displayed miko outfits one after another, inspected their tailoring in a white-hot fury, and hurled each one violently onto the tatami.
One had hakama so short they barely reached the upper thigh.
Another had a kosode with not merely the nape but the entire back laid bare.
And then there was one with fabric so impossibly sheer that not only the wearer's figure but even the color of their skin would be visible...?!
If my memory served correctly, miko outfits were most certainly not designed this way.
No -- these were something else entirely, were they not?!
``W-what do you think... Hanyuu?@k@vS16/46/DS43020233. Has any caught your eye?``
So said Riku as he hauled himself up from below the veranda... blood streaming freely from his nose.
With a smile of perfect, guileless innocence.
But... but! This time, and this time only... this time aloooone...!!

``Incidentally... Riku?@k@vS16/45/DS43070523. Every single one of these outfits fits my measurements precisely, even in the most... particular areas.@k@vS16/45/DS43070524. You would not happen to have...?``
``Indeed!@k@vS16/46/DS43020235. From the very day I resolved to attend to your needs, I enlisted the aid of like-minded comrades in the village and castle town to procure them!``
``?!@k@vS16/45/DS43070526. O-oh, is that so...?@k@vS16/45/DS43070527. A-and pray tell, how exactly did you determine my measurements...?``

``Hahaha!@k@vS16/46/DS43020237. That happens to be one of my special talents, you see.@k@vS16/46/DS43020238. I can gauge a captivating woman's exact proportions by sight alone!@k@vS16/46/DS43020239. Ah, and incidentally, your measurements are--``
``...Riku.@k@vS16/45/DS43070529. Come to think of it, I once learned a term on the mainland that is most fitting for circumstances such as these.``
``You PERVERT!!!``
``Gwuaghghaaah?!``
I may, however, have put slightly too much force into it... as Riku was unable to rise from the floor for quite some time afterward.
Dinner... was chikuzen-ni stew once again.
